title: Explosion-proof apparatus
abstract: According to an aspect of an example embodiment, there is provided an explosion-proof apparatus including a stopper formed in a hollow cylindrical shape that is open at a first side and closed at a second side opposite to the first side, the stopper being configured to be combined with an electrolytic condenser by surrounding an explosion-proof face of the electrolytic condenser and a lateral side of the electrolytic condenser connected to the explosion-proof face, through the first side of the stopper, and a holder provided on the stopper and configured to support the stopper to be combined to the electrolytic condenser, wherein the first side of the stopper is spaced apart from the explosion-proof face of the electrolytic condenser, wherein a lateral side of the stopper is formed in a stepped structure and includes a periphery of a first region of the lateral side of the stopper connected to the first side of the stopper which is larger than a periphery of a second region of the lateral side of the stopper connected to the second side of the stopper, and wherein the holder is provided on the stopper to surround at least part of the periphery of the second region of the stopper.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) An explosion-proof apparatus comprising: a stopper having a hollow cylindrical shape that is open at a first side and closed at a second side opposite to the first side, and the stopper being configured to be combined with an electrolytic condenser to surroundby surrounding an explosion-proof face of the electrolytic condenser and a lateral side of the electrolytic condenser connectingconnected to the explosion-proof face, through the first side of the stopper; and a holder mountedprovided on the stopper and configured to support the stopper to prevent the stopper from being separated frombe combined to the electrolytic condenser, wherein the first side of the stopper is spaced apart from the explosion-proof face of the electrolytic condenser, wherein a lateral side of the stopper has a stepped structure in which a periphery of a first region of the lateral side of the stopper connectingconnected to the first side of the stopper exceedswhich is larger than a periphery of a second region of the lateral side of the stopper connectingconnected to the second side of the stopper, and wherein the holder is mountedprovided on the stopper to surround at least part of the periphery of the second region of the stopper. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 1, wherein the stopper includescomprises: at least one hole formed provided in at least one offrom among the lateral side and the second side of the stopper, and wherein the at least one hole is smaller in size than the explosion-proof face of the electrolytic condenser. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 2, wherein if the stopper includescomprises a plurality of holes, and one of the plurality of holes is larger in size than the other holes. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 1, wherein the electrolytic condenser, the stopper, and the holder are positioned configured to be provided in a positioning hole formed in a printed circuit board if the electrolytic condenser is mounted on the printed circuit board, and wherein the holder includescomprises: a body configured to surround the outsidelateral side of the stopper; a plurality of first protrusions protruding from the outside of the body and configured to makebe in contact with opposite sides of the positioning hole to apply a pressing force downward to the printed circuit board; and a plurality of second protrusions protruding from the outside of the body and configured to makebe in contact with the opposite sides of the positioning hole to apply a pressing force upward to the printed circuit board. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 4, wherein the plurality of first protrusions are configured such that a first end of each of the plurality of the first protrusions furthest from the body is closer to the printed circuit board than a second end of each of the plurality of the first protrusions adjacent to the body when the electrolytic condenser, the stopper, and the holder are positioned provided in the positioning hole. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 4, wherein the plurality of second protrusions are configured to move toward each other while the electrolytic condenser, the stopper, and the holder are being positioned in the positioning hole, and to move away from each other after the electrolytic condenser, the stopper, and the holder are being positioned in the positioning hole. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 4, wherein the plurality of second protrusions includecomprise: opposite end protrusions formed on opposite ends of the body and configured to makebe in contact with a first housing located aboveon one side of the printed circuit board; and a central protrusion provided between the opposite end protrusions and configured to makebe in contact with a second housing located belowon another side the printed circuit board. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 4, wherein the plurality of second protrusions are configured such that a region relatively fara first end of each of the plurality of second protrusions furthest away from the body is wider than a region relatively closea second end of each of the plurality of second protrusions closest to the body. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 4, wherein the positioning hole has a cross shape, and the electrolytic condenser, the stopper, and the holder are positioned provided in the positioning hole in a longitudinal direction of the cross shape, and wherein the plurality of first protrusions and the plurality of second protrusions are configured to be inmake contact with opposite sides of the cross shape in a lateral direction of the cross shape ifwhen the electrolytic condenser, the stopper, and the holder are positionedprovided in the positioning hole. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 4, wherein the holder further includescomprises: at least one third protrusion protruding from the outside of the body, and wherein opposite endsan end of the at least one third protrusion make is configured to be in contact with at least one housing configured to secure the printed circuit board. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 10, wherein the body has a "u"U shape that surrounds a portion of the outsidelateral side of the stopper, and wherein the at least one third protrusion includescomprises: a central protrusion provided between the plurality of second protrusions and configured to be inmake contact with a first housing located below one side of the printed circuit board; and opposite end protrusions formed on opposite ends of the body and configured to makebe in contact with a second housing located aboveon another side the printed circuit board. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 1, wherein the electrolytic condenser has comprises a spiral groove formed provided on the lateral side connectingconnected to the explosion-proof face of the electrolytic condenser, wherein the stopper hascomprises a spiral protrusion corresponding to the spiral groove, and wherein the electrolytic condenser and the stopper are combined with each other by using the spiral groove and the spiral protrusion. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: a cover member configured to surround the stopper and the lateral side of the electrolytic condenser not being combined with the stopper, wherein the cover member is coupled with the electrolytic condenser to secure the stopper to the electrolytic condenser. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 13, wherein the cover member is coupled with a portion of the electrolytic condenser not being covered with cover vinyl, and is formed of a polymer and on which specification information of the electrolytic condenser is marked. The explosion-proof apparatus of claim 13, wherein the cover member is further configured to additionally surround a bottom side, opposite to the explosion-proof face, of the electrolytic condenser.
